title: teucrium coeleste nothosubsp. schreberi
intro: Teucrium coeleste nothosubsp. schreberi, also known as mountain germander, is a subspecies of Teucrium coeleste, a perennial plant native to the Balkan Peninsula. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent, due to the presence of essential oils such as borneol and camphor. The health benefits of Teucrium coeleste nothosubsp. schreberi include its ability to aid digestion, reduce fever, and soothe respiratory issues, making it a valuable herb in traditional folk medicine. Herbal preparations of Teucrium coeleste nothosubsp. schreberi include tea, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
